name: Cloudflare
description: Builds core networking infrastructure in Rust. Pingora is their open-source proxy framework; quiche implements QUIC and HTTP/3; boringtun is a WireGuard VPN kernel bypass.

name: Mozilla
description: Originated Rust. Uses it throughout Firefox's rendering engine, sccache for distributed compilation, and Uniffi for cross-platform API bindings.

name: AWS
description: Ships Rust in production for Firecracker (microVM hypervisor), the AWS SDK for Rust, and LLRT (low-latency JavaScript runtime). Multiple teams maintain open-source Rust tooling under awslabs.

name: Microsoft
description: Invests in Rust for Windows kernel integration, the windows-rs bindings crate, and their edit CLI tool. Hosts the widely-used Rust Training curriculum.

name: Google
description: Uses Rust in Android platform code, the Fuchsia OS, and ChromeOS. Publishes Comprehensive Rust — the official course used internally to onboard engineers.

name: Discord
description: Switched from Go to Rust for their Read States service, eliminating latency spikes caused by garbage collection pauses. Published as a case study in production Rust adoption.

name: Dropbox
description: Rewrote core sync engine components in Rust. Early large-scale production adopter; the rewrite enabled significant performance improvements in their desktop client.

name: 1Password
description: Uses Rust for their cross-platform secrets management core. The 1Password 8 client shares a single Rust codebase across desktop, browser extension, and CLI targets.

name: Oxide Computer
description: Builds a rack-scale cloud computer where the entire stack — firmware, OS, hypervisor, and management plane — is written in Rust. Hubris is their open-source RTOS.

name: Parity Technologies
description: Builds the Polkadot SDK and Substrate blockchain framework in Rust. One of the largest Rust codebases in the blockchain space.

name: Deno
description: JavaScript/TypeScript runtime built in Rust. The Deno core and V8 bindings (rusty_v8) are open source; Deno Deploy runs on a Rust-powered edge network.

name: Zed Industries
description: High-performance code editor written entirely in Rust. Their custom GPU-accelerated UI framework GPUI is open source and powers the editor from scratch.

name: Fly.io
description: Application deployment platform. Uses Rust for Corrosion — their distributed SQLite replication system — and in edge networking components.

name: Turso
description: Edge-first SQLite database built on libSQL, a Rust-maintained fork of SQLite. Rust powers both the server runtime and the client SDKs.

name: PingCAP
description: Builds TiKV, the distributed key-value store underlying TiDB, in Rust. One of the first major production databases written in Rust.

name: SurrealDB
description: Multi-model database engine written entirely in Rust. Supports SQL, graph, document, and key-value queries from a single engine with embedded and server modes.

name: Embark Studios
description: Game studio betting on Rust for the entire games stack. Open-sources GPU compute tooling, asset pipelines, and Rust game engine components.

name: Ferrous Systems
description: Builds Ferrocene, the safety-qualified Rust toolchain certified for use in automotive, medical, and aerospace applications. Leading commercial Rust consultancy.

name: Buoyant
description: Company behind the Linkerd service mesh. The Linkerd2-proxy data plane is written in Rust using Tokio and Hyper, replacing the original Scala implementation.

name: Vercel
description: Uses Rust for Turbopack (incremental JavaScript bundler) and Turborepo (monorepo build orchestrator). Both are open source and production-grade.

name: The Rust Project
description: The official Rust programming language organization. Maintains the compiler (rustc), standard library, Cargo package manager, and all core language infrastructure.

name: Fuel Labs
description: Builds Fuel, a modular execution layer for Ethereum. The Sway programming language, FuelVM, and Forc toolchain are all implemented in Rust.

name: Astral
description: Builds Python tooling in Rust. Ruff is an extremely fast Python linter; uv is a Rust-powered pip replacement. Both are open source and see wide adoption.

name: Tauri
description: Framework for building desktop applications with web frontends and Rust backends. Lighter and more secure than Electron. Fiscally hosted by the Commons Conservancy; all contributors are volunteers.

name: Bun
description: Fast all-in-one JavaScript runtime. The core runtime is written in Zig and C, but bundles significant Rust-implemented components for HTTP, SQL, and process management.

name: OpenAI
description: Uses Rust in systems and tooling. The Codex CLI — the open-source AI coding assistant — is built in Rust and is one of the most-starred Rust repositories on GitHub.

name: Union
description: Builds a trust-minimized interoperability protocol. The Union node and proving infrastructure are written in Rust.

name: Warp
description: GPU-accelerated terminal with AI integration, built entirely in Rust. Uses a custom rendering pipeline and the wgpu graphics library.

name: Alacritty
description: Cross-platform, GPU-accelerated terminal emulator. One of the pioneering Rust desktop applications; entirely open source under the Apache 2.0 license.

name: Starship
description: Minimal, fast, and customizable shell prompt written in Rust. Runs on any shell and any operating system; used by hundreds of thousands of developers.

name: Meilisearch
description: Open-source, fast full-text search engine with a focus on developer experience. The search core, ranking algorithm, and all official SDKs are open source.

name: Rerun
description: Multimodal data logging and visualization platform for robotics and Physical AI. The core engine and SDK are written in Rust; used by teams building autonomous systems, computer vision pipelines, and sensor fusion.

name: Radar Labs
description: Developer-first location infrastructure processing over 1 billion API calls per day. Rust powers the core SDK and data pipeline; TypeScript handles the API layer.

name: FUTO
description: Technology organization building open, privacy-respecting alternatives to mainstream platforms. Polycentric is their decentralized social protocol — a federated, cryptographically-verified content graph implemented in Rust.

name: Kepler
description: Builds a trust and verification layer for AI agents in high-stakes industries, starting with finance. Rust backend powers the extraction pipelines and agent orchestration infrastructure.

name: yeet
description: Building a dynamic runtime on top of the Linux BPF subsystem. Targets low-level infrastructure where Rust's systems-programming capabilities and safety model are essential.

name: Tokio
description: The Tokio Project maintains the async runtime ecosystem for Rust: tokio (the runtime), axum (web framework), tower (service abstractions), tracing (observability), and mio (I/O event loop). The foundation of most production Rust services.

name: Bevy Engine
description: Open source, data-driven game engine built entirely in Rust. The most active Rust game engine project, with a modular ECS architecture and a large contributor community.

name: Bytecode Alliance
description: Industry nonprofit (members include Microsoft, Intel, Arm, Fastly, Mozilla) advancing WebAssembly and WASI. Maintains wasmtime (the reference Wasm runtime), cranelift (code generator), and the component model spec.

name: Linebender
description: Research collective building next-generation Rust UI infrastructure. Projects include Vello (GPU 2D renderer), Xilem (native UI architecture), Parley (rich text layout), and Peniko (rendering primitives). Primarily funded by Google.

name: Nushell
description: Nu is a new type of shell written in Rust. Structured data pipelines, cross-platform, with a strong plugin ecosystem.

name: Qdrant
description: Vector similarity search engine and database written entirely in Rust. Purpose-built for AI embeddings and semantic search at scale. Qdrant the company offers managed cloud; the core engine is open source.

name: Polars
description: Blazing-fast dataframe library written in Rust with bindings for Python, Node.js, and R. The fastest-growing Rust data library. Polars Inc. was founded in 2023 to provide commercial support.

name: Quickwit
description: Rust search and observability infrastructure: tantivy (fast full-text search library) and quickwit (cloud-native distributed search engine). Team joined Datadog in January 2025.

name: Datadog
description: Cloud monitoring, security, and analytics platform. Acquired Quickwit (Rust distributed search) in January 2025 to accelerate their distributed logging infrastructure.

name: Anthropic
description: AI safety company and maker of Claude. Acquired Bun (Oven-sh JavaScript runtime) in December 2025. The Bun runtime is undergoing a Zig-to-Rust rewrite as of 2026.

name: Hyperium
description: hyper, h2, http, and h3 — the HTTP primitive layer underlying axum, reqwest, and most of the Rust HTTP ecosystem. Referenced by four corpus funding programs.

name: Embassy
description: Async embedded Rust framework enabling async/await on microcontrollers. The dominant async embedded project in the corpus. NGI Zero funded.

name: Actix
description: actix-web — the most downloaded Rust web framework, consistently topping TechEmpower performance benchmarks. Community-maintained with no corporate parent.

name: gfx-rs
description: wgpu — cross-platform GPU API implementing WebGPU in Rust. The rendering backend used by Bevy, Dioxus, Iced, and Zed.

name: probe-rs
description: Embedded debugging and flashing toolkit for Rust. NGI Zero funded; referenced in nlnet-ngi-zero funding records for embedded Rust toolchain support.

name: Helix
description: Post-modern modal text editor written entirely in Rust. Tree-sitter and LSP native from the ground up. Community-maintained with 44k+ stars.

name: Zellij
description: Terminal workspace and multiplexer written in Rust with a WASM plugin system enabling arbitrary language plugins. Community-maintained with 33k+ stars.

name: PyO3
description: pyo3 and maturin — the standard Python↔Rust interoperability toolkit. Used by Polars, HuggingFace tokenizers, orjson, and hundreds of Rust libraries exposing Python bindings.

name: Yew
description: React-inspired frontend framework for building web applications in Rust compiled to WebAssembly. One of the oldest and most mature Rust WASM UI frameworks.

name: Iced
description: Elm-inspired cross-platform GUI library for Rust. Runs natively on desktop and in the browser via WASM. 30k+ stars.

name: Ratatui
description: Terminal UI library forked from tui-rs. The de facto toolkit for building TUI applications in Rust. 22k+ stars.

name: uutils
description: Cross-platform GNU coreutils rewrite in Rust. Drop-in replacement for ls, cp, cat, and 100+ other core utilities on macOS, Linux, and Windows. 24k+ stars.

name: Leptos
description: Reactive fullstack Rust web framework with SSR and WASM hydration. Uses axum as the server backend. 20k+ stars.

name: Pocket
description: YC W26 company building personal hardware devices with Tauri desktop applications. Shipped 30,000+ units. Rust backend powers Tauri plugins, system commands, and hardware integration.

name: Wasmer
description: Builds the leading standalone WebAssembly runtime in Rust. wasmer (20k+ stars) supports LLVM, Cranelift, and Singlepass backends and runs Wasm in servers, edge, and embedded contexts.

name: Typst
description: Modern typesetting system written entirely in Rust, positioned as a LaTeX alternative. typst (54k+ stars) compiles to PDF with a live-preview workflow. Berlin-based.

name: Tracel AI
description: Builds Burn, the Rust-native deep learning framework (15k+ stars), and cubecl, a unified compute language for GPU programming in Rust. Focused on training and inference performance.

name: smol async
description: 23 async primitive crates forming an alternative async runtime ecosystem outside Tokio: async-channel, polling, async-io, async-task, async-std, and more.

name: Rust CLI WG
description: Rust CLI working group. Maintains config-rs, human-panic, env_logger, confy, and 8 more CLI utility crates used across the Rust ecosystem.

name: libp2p
description: Modular P2P networking stack in Rust. Used by IPFS, Ethereum clients, Polkadot, and other decentralized protocol implementations.

name: Serde
description: serde and serde_json — the universal serialization framework used by virtually every Rust project in the corpus. serde_json is the most downloaded Rust crate on crates.io.

name: Diesel
description: Diesel ORM — safe, extensible query builder and ORM for Rust. The primary Rust ORM used in production database applications. 14k+ stars.

name: Rayon
description: Data-parallelism library for Rust. The standard parallel iterator library used by Polars, ripgrep, and most performance-critical Rust tools. 13k+ stars.

name: Clap
description: Command-line argument parser for Rust. The most widely used Rust CLI argument parsing library by download count. 16k+ stars.

name: wasm-bindgen
description: JS↔Rust WASM interop glue layer. Required by virtually every Rust-to-WASM project targeting the browser. Maintained by the rustwasm working group.

name: RustCrypto
description: Collaborative collection of cryptographic algorithm implementations in pure Rust. Covers AEADs, digital signatures, hash functions, key derivation, elliptic curves, and X.509 certificate formats. No unsafe code; no_std compatible throughout. NLnet NGI Assure funded.

name: rustls
description: Memory-safe TLS implementation in pure Rust. The primary alternative to OpenSSL for Rust network applications. Funded by ISRG Prossimo, NLnet NGI Zero, and the Sovereign Tech Fund.

name: Servo
description: Independent web browser engine written in Rust, hosted by the Linux Foundation Europe. Originally created at Mozilla. Actively maintained as an embeddable web rendering engine. Funded by the Rust Foundation and NLnet.

name: esp-rs
description: Community organization maintaining Rust support for Espressif chips (ESP32, ESP32-S3, ESP32-C3). Covers bare-metal and ESP-IDF-based HAL crates, toolchain support, and no_std training materials. NLnet NGI Zero funded.

name: Paradigm
description: Crypto research and investment firm behind Reth, the modular Ethereum execution client written in Rust. Also the origin of Foundry (foundry-rs) and Alloy (alloy-rs), making Paradigm the largest single contributor to Rust Ethereum tooling.

name: Matrix.org
description: Open standard and reference implementations for decentralized, real-time communication. matrix-rust-sdk is the Rust client library underpinning Element X and other Matrix clients. Funded by NLnet NGI Commons and the Sovereign Tech Fund.
